en.name: Troponin T in blood
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context Cardiac troponin T (cTnT) is a regulatory protein of the cardiac muscle thin filament, released into the circulation when cardiomyocytes are damaged. Together with cardiac troponin I (cTnI), it is the central biomarker for the diagnosis of myocardial injury and acute myocardial infarction [1]. cTnT may also be elevated in many other settings of cardiomyocyte stress (heart failure, pulmonary embolism, sepsis, chronic kidney disease). The source data will typically be a single numeric result per patient-timestamp, produced by immunoassay. Results are reported as a mass concentration, conventionally in ng/L for high-sensitivity assays and ng/mL for older contemporary assays. `1 ng/mL = 1000 ng/L`. **Note: high-sensitivity cTnT (hs-cTnT) vs contemporary cTnT.** High-sensitivity assays measure detectable cTn values in > 50% of healthy individuals, where contemporary assays cannot ; the 99th-percentile URL is assay-specific and must be looked up in the manufacturer documentation [1]. Values from different assays are not interchangeable. Both contemporary and hs-cTnT codes are included in this concept set ; analyses that distinguish them must use the LOINC code and the source unit (ng/L vs ng/mL), not the value alone. **Note: cTnT vs cTnI.** Cardiac troponin T (cTnT) and cardiac troponin I (cTnI) are two distinct gene products. Both are cardiac-specific and clinically equivalent for the diagnosis of myocardial injury, but they are **not numerically interchangeable** and use different cut-offs. Most institutions use one or the other. This concept set covers **cTnT only**. ## Included Concepts The set is anchored on four LOINC Hierarchy concepts (scoped to `Chemistry - non-challenge`), each expanded to include all their descendants. The set resolves to six LOINC "Lab Test" concepts, grouped here by specimen. ### Serum or plasma - *3019800 / 6598-7 — Troponin T.cardiac [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma (LOINC)*: most widely used (older standard assays). - *40769783 / 67151-1 — Troponin T.cardiac [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma by High sensitivity method (LOINC)*: hs-cTnT, current standard. ### Whole blood - *3048529 / 48425-3 — Troponin T.cardiac [Mass/volume] in Blood (LOINC)* - *1469858 / 106734-7 — Troponin T.cardiac [Mass/volume] in Serum, Plasma or Blood by Rapid immunoassay (LOINC)*: point-of-care. ### Venous blood - *3019572 / 6597-9 — Troponin T.cardiac [Mass/volume] in Venous blood (LOINC)* **Note on units.** Recommended unit follows LOINC's `EXAMPLE_UCUM_UNITS`: **`ng/L`** (concept 8842 — nanogram per litre) for high-sensitivity assays, **`ng/mL`** (concept 8842 — nanogram per millilitre) for standard assays. `1 ng/mL = 1000 ng/L`. ## Excluded Concepts The following LOINC concepts in Blood / Serum or Plasma are deliberately excluded by name: - *3052931 / 48426-1 — Troponin T.cardiac [Presence] in Blood*: qualitative, not a concentration. - *3042837 / 33204-9 — Troponin T.cardiac [Presence] in Serum or Plasma*: qualitative, not a concentration. - *36304662 / 89575-5 — Troponin T.cardiac [Interpretation] in Serum or Plasma Qualitative by High sensitivity method*: qualitative interpretation, not a concentration. - *3965306 / 101915-7 — Troponin T.cardiac [Mass/volume] in 6 hour Serum or Plasma*: timed-protocol variant (Δ-troponin at 6 h), out of scope for a raw-concentration concept set. In addition, the set deliberately omits: - **Cord blood and fetus specimens**: physiologically distinct from post-natal blood. ## Mapping Notes - **Default concept** when the source reports a troponin T value: `3019800 / 6598-7 — Troponin T.cardiac [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma`. - **Source explicitly says "high sensitivity" or unit is ng/L**: map to `40769783 / 67151-1 — ... by High sensitivity method`. - **Source reports a qualitative result (positive/negative)**: do **not** map. Qualitative results are out of scope. - **Source reports troponin I (cTnI)**: do **not** map to this concept set. Use a dedicated cTnI concept set instead (if defined). - **Source reports a Δ-troponin (change at 1 h, 3 h, 6 h)**: do **not** map. Δ-troponin is a derived measurement. - **Do not confuse with**: troponin I (cTnI, different molecule and cut-offs), CK-MB, myoglobin (different cardiac markers). ## References [1] Thygesen K, Alpert JS, Jaffe AS, Chaitman BR, Bax JJ, Morrow DA, et al. Fourth Universal Definition of Myocardial Infarction (2018). *Circulation*. 2018;138(20):e618-51. Available: https://doi.org/10.1161/CIR.0000000000000617 [2] Regenstrief Institute. LOINC code 67151-1 — Troponin T.cardiac [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma by High sensitivity method. Available: https://loinc.org/67151-1/
